---
title: "Git & GitHub essentials"
subtitle: "Topic 1 · Day 1 · 1 hour"
date: last-modified
---

## Why version control?

- Keep a full history of every change (who, what, when, why).
- Collaborate without emailing `analysis_final_v3_reallyfinal.R`.
- Revert safely when an experiment breaks your code.
- Publish work reproducibly.

::: {.callout-note}
In this course **every exercise you submit lives in a Git repository
on GitHub**. You need the workflow working by the end of this hour.
:::

## Git vs GitHub

|  | Git | GitHub |
|---|---|---|
| What | A local tool that tracks changes | A web service that hosts Git repos |
| Where | Your laptop | github.com |
| Needed? | Always | To share with others |

## Setup checklist {.smaller}

1. **Install Git** · <https://git-scm.com/downloads>
2. **Configure identity**
   ```bash
   git config --global user.name  "Your Name"
   git config --global user.email "you@ulpgc.es"
   ```
3. **Create a GitHub account** · <https://github.com/signup>
4. **Authenticate** via a Personal Access Token
   ```r
   install.packages("usethis")
   usethis::create_github_token()
   gitcreds::gitcreds_set()   # paste the token
   ```
5. **Check from RStudio** that the *Git* pane appears.

## Core vocabulary

::: {.incremental}
- **Repository** — a project folder tracked by Git.
- **Commit** — a saved snapshot with a message.
- **Branch** — an independent line of development.
- **Remote** — a copy hosted elsewhere (e.g. GitHub).
- **Fork** — your personal copy of someone else's repo on GitHub.
- **Pull Request (PR)** — proposal to merge your changes into the original.
:::

## The course workflow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[chrglez/quantitative-methods-master-tides] -->|fork| B[yourname/quantitative-methods-master-tides]
  B -->|clone| C[your laptop]
  C -->|commit + push| B
  B -->|submission URL| D[instructor]
```

1. **Fork** the course repo on GitHub.
2. **Clone** your fork to your laptop.
3. For each exercise: open the template, solve it, `commit`, `push`.
4. Paste your fork URL (or PR link) into the submission form.

## Live demo · the four commands you will use every day

```bash
# inside your cloned project folder
git status                     # what changed?
git add  exercises/day1/       # stage specific files
git commit -m "Day 1 solution" # snapshot with a message
git push                       # upload to your GitHub fork
```

## Common pitfalls

- **Never** commit `.Rdata`, `.RData` or `.Rhistory` — the course
  `.gitignore` already excludes them.
- **Never** force-push (`git push --force`) to a shared branch.
- If you get `Authentication failed`, regenerate your PAT with
  `usethis::create_github_token()`.
- Large data files (>50 MB) do **not** belong in Git. Put them under
  `datasets/raw/` (which is gitignored) and commit only the
  `download.R` script.

## Resources

- [Happy Git with R](https://happygitwithr.com/) — Jenny Bryan's canonical guide.
- [GitHub Skills](https://skills.github.com/) — short interactive tracks.
- The [`exercises/`](../exercises/) folder in this repo.

# Your turn {background-color="#0067a2"}

Fork the course repo, clone it locally, open it in RStudio, make one
commit that adds your name to `exercises/day1/README.md`, and push.
